items.headline: CME chief executive says company plans to sue CFTC after perpetual futures approval
items.subheadline: Kalshi's perpetual futures product did not meet the Dodd-Frank Act's definition of a "swap" and should not have been approved, Terrence Duffy said.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: MTSR news: Strategy's STRC preferred stock drops to a record low $89
items.subheadline: The slide has paused the above-par share sales Strategy uses to fund bitcoin purchases, and it is the same stock whose dividends forced the company's first BTC sale this month.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live: Bitcoin and ether ETFs lost $111 million combined as rate-cut hopes died
items.subheadline: Total crypto market value has held steady near $2.26 trillion since Tuesday, with the recovery losing momentum after the Fed killed rate-cut hopes and spot ETFs swung back to outflows.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: The bond market is flashing a clear signal on interest rates. Bitcoin bulls should take note
items.subheadline: The bond market is sending a signal that complicates prospects of a near-term bitcoin bull run.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
items.subheadline: The MFSA is seeking feedback on whether decentralization should be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary concept.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: FTX's Sam Bankman-Fried loses appeal of criminal conviction on fraud, conspiracy charges
items.subheadline: The onetime FTX CEO did not persuade a panel of judges that his trial was unfair, the appeals court panel ruled Friday.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Aerodrome is turning liquidity into a prediction market with its biggest upgrade yet
items.subheadline: The concept, called Predictive Allocation, will encourage participants to anticipate where liquidity will be needed next, instead of rewarding them for directing incentives toward pools that have already generated fees.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: This bitcoin level has historically meant over 100% median returns, Kraken says
items.subheadline: Bitcoin's recent dips below its 200-week moving average have historically produced median returns over 100% within a year, with minimal drawdown pain for buyers, according to Kraken.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Hyperliquid loses Anthropic, OpenAI markets as creator shuts down project
items.subheadline: Ventuals, one of the teams behind real-world asset perpetuals on Hyperliquid, said it is closing its markets and joining another project in the ecosystem.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: State Street targets stablecoin reserve boom with new money market fund
items.subheadline: The asset manager joins BlackRock, Franklin Templeton and others competing to manage reserves backing the growing stablecoin market.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Coinbase joins tokenized stock race with onchain shares and dividend payments
items.subheadline: The crypto exchange said investors will own the shares and receive dividends, underscoring growing momentum behind tokenized securities.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: XRP News: Ripple invests in Flutterwave, bringing RLUSD and XRP Ledger to payments in Africa
items.subheadline: The deal values Flutterwave at $3.2 billion, and will integrate Ripple's RLUSD and the XRP Ledger network to speed cross-border payments across Africa.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Coinbase introduces AI adviser, stock options and pre-IPO markets in finance push
items.subheadline: The crypto exchange is expanding into investing, derivatives and banking services as it pursues an all-in-one financial platform.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: BitGo offers MiCA compliance lifeline to EU crypto firms as license deadline looms
items.subheadline: BaFin-regulated BitGo says its Crypto-as-a-Service platform can help eligible crypto players across Europe an alternative way to navigate MiCA.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Startup drops first universal AI agent payment plug into Asia's $28.9 trillion ecommerce market
items.subheadline: While legacy banks keep corporate money idled in slow regional accounts, a new protocol allows software systems to settle multi-currency trade instantly.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Fifth-generation Kanoo group member is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
items.subheadline: Member of 135-year-old group is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Mexican billionaire with 70% of his investment portfolio in bitcoin says it's better than real estate
items.subheadline: Ricardo Salinas Pliego, whose net worth is estimated at roughly $5 billion, is a hardcore bitcoin maximalist who told his wife to mortgage her house to buy bitcoin.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Solana news: Moody’s rolls out credit ratings onchain in tokenized asset push
items.subheadline: The ratings giant is embedding credit scores directly into blockchain-based securities, a move aimed at boosting institutional adoption.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Here is how Coinbase plan to survive the crypto winter by ditching its reliance on trading fees
items.subheadline: Analysts say the exchange's push into derivatives, payments and infrastructure broadens its growth story beyond crypto trading.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: XRP slips 4% below $1.20 after breakout rally stalls near key resistance
items.subheadline: Heavy selling pushed XRP back through a closely watched support level, though buyers stepped in above $1.17 to prevent a deeper pullback.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin traders don't expect BTC to cross $75,000 as U.S.-Iran threats remain
items.subheadline: Monetary policy may be back in focus, with Kevin Warsh's first policy meeting as Fed chairman coming this week.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin ETFs bled cash Monday while every other crypto ETF gained
items.subheadline: Ether, XRP, Solana and Hyperliquid funds all took in money, but bitcoin's outflow was really just Grayscale's GBTC.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: This AI builds a business, runs it and settles payments in USDC
items.subheadline: Text an idea. Get a business. That describes Y Combinator's latest AI.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Glamsterdam upgrade moves into its final development stage
items.subheadline: Developers are entering the final stretch of work on Glamsterdam, the network's next major upgrade, as teams begin testing a version of the fork in a closed environment.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin bottom signal flashes as holders absorbed 125,000 BTC in June
items.subheadline: Bitcoin's Sharpe ratio hit a level that has marked every cycle low since 2015, but in each case it preceded months of basing rather than an immediate rebound.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: TAO news: A new Bittensor proposal would turn validators into something like fund managers
items.subheadline: Root Reborn would have TAO validators pick which subnets to back and reinvest yield into, rather than constantly selling subnet tokens to pay stakers. It is a code proposal in review, not a live change.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: FIFA wanted Avalanche's blockchain to help curb World Cup ticket scalping. Here's how it's going
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at how FIFA is using the Avalanche blockchain to test out a new ticketing system.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) layer-2s face a bear-market reality check
items.subheadline: Botanix's shutdown has raised an uncomfortable question for Bitcoin builders: Does the market actually want programmable BTC, or just a better way to borrow, lend and earn yield on it?
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: Kalshi’s crypto perpetuals spark debate over whether they’re futures or swaps
items.subheadline: A clash between derivatives veterans is exposing a deeper fight over how U.S. regulators should classify crypto perpetual contracts.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Summer of crypto (regs): State of Crypto
items.subheadline: Lawmakers are debating taxes, the CFTC put out a prediction market proposal and court cases are heating up.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Binance says its European regulatory application is compliant despite report of Greek rejection
items.subheadline: An EU crypto license application by the world’s largest crypto exchange is set to be rejected by the Greek regulator HCMC, according to a report from Reuters.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: U.S. senators urge Treasury not to leave states out of GENIUS Act stablecoin process
items.subheadline: Senators led by Republican Cynthia Lummis are insisting the Treasury Department ensure states get a process to prove their ability to supervise stablecoins.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto PAC's $12 million Senate candidate, Barry Moore, wins Alabama GOP primary
items.subheadline: Crypto PAC poured $12 million into Barry Moore’s Alabama Senate bid, and it worked: the Trump loyalist crushed former Navy SEAL Jared Hudson in the GOP primary runoff, setting him up as the likely nominee in November.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto industry aghast at Illinois' new tax on holding or transferring digital assets in state budget
items.subheadline: The 0.2% tax on any business activity involving digital assets was added last-minute, and is unlikely to change, two people familiar with the matter said.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Kentucky targets prediction markets, puts Trump-aligned state in potential clash with president's team
items.subheadline: President Donald Trump has taken the stance that states have no business with firms like Kalshi and Polymarket, and now a staunch GOP state is defying that view.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto market positioning is 'defensive and thin' after Fed rate meeting, Marex  analysts say
items.subheadline: While the Federal Reserve held interest rates, Chair Kevin Warsh made it clear the U.S. central bank is more worried about inflation than growth.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Aster (ASTER) popped over 10% on radical 'buyback and burn' upgrade. But gains were short-lived
items.subheadline: ASTER sees two-way volatility as bullish protocol-specific news meets hawkish Fed.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Bitcoin's (BTC) nemesis, the Dollar Index (DXY), is on the verge of a major breakout: Daybook: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: Your day-ahead look for June 18, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Mounting AI costs and weaker performance are driving investors toward AI infrastructure
items.subheadline: Capital is flowing out of the largest tech companies and bitcoin as investors pile into semiconductors, memory stocks and space-related opportunities.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Hive gains 10% after securing Canada sovereign AI contract with Bell Canada
items.subheadline: GPU cloud contract with Bell and Cohere strengthens HIVE's shift from bitcoin mining to high performance AI computing.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Alchemy's AI-driven AgentCard gains access to Visa payments network
items.subheadline: The AgentCard integration with Visa Intelligent Commerce allows AI agents built on models from any provider, including OpenAI or Anthropic, to effect commercial transactions.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Stellar (XLM) jumps 10% while index declines
items.subheadline: Internet Computer (ICP) fell 4.1% and Sui (SUI) dropped 4%, leading the index lower from Wednesday.
items.author: CoinDesk Indices

items.headline: Algorand unveils roadmap for post-quantum security by end-2027
items.subheadline: The announcement reflects a growing recognition across the crypto industry that transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography could take years, requiring changes not only to user wallets but also to core protocol infrastructure.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Crypto ETFs: Why bitcoin investors should trade the cycle, not dollar-cost average
items.subheadline: Bitcoin’s 4-year cycle makes DCA costly. Learn why a cycle-smart strategy is essential for advisors to better manage volatility and maximize client returns.
items.author: Markus Thielen

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Foundation loses another key leader as Hsiao-Wei Wang resigns
items.subheadline: Wang's departure follows the resignation of fellow co-executive director Tomasz Stańczak and marks the latest in a string of high-profile exits at the EF.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act rule
items.subheadline: The Federal Reserve, Treasury and other regulators have issued a proposed rule that would set identification standards, and it's now open for public comments.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
items.subheadline: Alexander Mashinsky, the founder of failed crypto lender Celsius, had earlier been imprisoned for fraud and is now formally banned from CFTC registration.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: BTC, ETH, SOL price news: Bitcoin falls below $63,000 as risk assets sell off
items.subheadline: Crypto dropped across the board on Friday in holiday-thinned trading, giving back the week's gains. With oil down 9% and the Iran deal signed, the question turns to whether this cycle gets an altseason at all.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin traders load up on bearish bets all the way down to $52,000
items.subheadline: Bitcoin traders are scrambling to buy options bets that would pay off if the selloff deepens.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin has traded below its mining cost for five months, squeezing miners
items.subheadline: About 20% of miners are now unprofitable, and publicly traded miners sold more than 32,000 bitcoin in the first quarter to cover operating costs, more than they offloaded in all of 2025.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price news: Ripple-linked token falls 3% after losing $1.15 support
items.subheadline: Heavy selling pushed XRP back below a key support zone, reinforcing a downtrend that has repeatedly stalled rallies near $1.25.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
